searchResults.snippet: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) Policy 1. Purpose This policy ensures compliance with the Federal Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) and provides eligible employees of the City of Evergreen with job-protected leave for qualifying family and medical reasons. It also outlines employee rights, responsibilities, and procedures for requesting leave. 2. Eligibility Employees are eligible if they: · Have worked for the City for 12 months (not necessarily consecutive). · Have completed 1,250 hours of service in the preceding 12 months. · Work at a location where the City employs 50 or more employees within 75 miles . 3. Qualifying Reasons for Leave Eligible employees may take up to 12 weeks of unpaid leave in a 12-month period for: · Birth and care of a newborn child. · Placement of a child for adoption or foster care. · Care for a spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition. · Employee’s own serious health condition preventing job performance. · Qualifying exigency related to a family member’s military service. Military Caregiver Leave: Up to 26 weeks to care for a covered service member with a serious injury or illness. 4. Definitions · Spouse: Includes common-law and same-sex marriages. · Child: Biological, adopted, foster, stepchild, legal ward, or a child for whom the employee stands in loco parentis. · Serious Health Condition: Illness or injury requiring inpatient care or continuing treatment by a health care provider. 5. Notice Requirements Employees must: · Provide 30 days’ notice for foreseeable leave. · Notify HR as soon as practicable for emergencies. · Submit required documentation (medical certification, adoption papers, etc.). 6. Benefits During Leave · Health insurance continues under the same terms. · Employees may use accrued paid leave concurrently with FMLA. · Job restoration to the same or equivalent position upon return. 7. Request Process 1. Complete the FMLA Request Form (see Appendix A). 2. Submit supporting documentation within 15 days . 3. HR will confirm eligibility and respond within 5 business days . 8. Intermittent or Reduced Schedule Leave Permitted when medically necessary. Employees must make reasonable efforts to schedule treatments to minimize disruption. 9. Employee Responsibilities · Provide timely notice and documentation. · Maintain communication with HR during leave. · Continue health insurance contributions. 10. City Responsibilities · Provide eligibility notice within 5 business days . · Maintain confidentiality of medical information. · Ensure non-retaliation and compliance with FMLA. 11. Enforcement Violations of FMLA rights may result in disciplinary action and legal remedies. Employees may file complaints with HR or the U.S. Department of Labor.

result.summary:
Yes, you can use FMLA to care for a loved one. Eligible employees may take up to 12 weeks of unpaid leave in a 12-month period for the following qualifying reasons:

- Care for a spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition.
- Birth and care of a newborn child.
- Placement of a child for adoption or foster care.
- Employee’s own serious health condition preventing job performance.
- Qualifying exigency related to a family member’s military service.

Additionally, employees may take up to 26 weeks to care for a covered service member with a serious injury or illness [1].

CitationSources.Name: FMLA Policy.docx
CitationSources.Text: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) Policy 1. Purpose This policy ensures compliance with the Federal Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) and provides eligible employees of the City of Evergreen with job-protected leave for qualifying family and medical reasons. It also outlines employee rights, responsibilities, and procedures for requesting leave. 2. Eligibility Employees are eligible if they: · Have worked for the City for 12 months (not necessarily consecutive). · Have completed 1,250 hours of service in the preceding 12 months. · Work at a location where the City employs 50 or more employees within 75 miles . 3. Qualifying Reasons for Leave Eligible employees may take up to 12 weeks of unpaid leave in a 12-month period for: · Birth and care of a newborn child. · Placement of a child for adoption or foster care. · Care for a spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition. · Employee’s own serious health condition preventing job performance. · Qualifying exigency related to a family member’s military service. Military Caregiver Leave: Up to 26 weeks to care for a covered service member with a serious injury or illness. 4. Definitions · Spouse: Includes common-law and same-sex marriages. · Child: Biological, adopted, foster, stepchild, legal ward, or a child for whom the employee stands in loco parentis. · Serious Health Condition: Illness or injury requiring inpatient care or continuing treatment by a health care provider. 5. Notice Requirements Employees must: · Provide 30 days’ notice for foreseeable leave. · Notify HR as soon as practicable for emergencies. · Submit required documentation (medical certification, adoption papers, etc.). 6. Benefits During Leave · Health insurance continues under the same terms. · Employees may use accrued paid leave concurrently with FMLA. · Job restoration to the same or equivalent position upon return. 7. Request Process 1. Complete the FMLA Request Form (see Appendix A). 2. Submit supporting documentation within 15 days . 3. HR will confirm eligibility and respond within 5 business days . 8. Intermittent or Reduced Schedule Leave Permitted when medically necessary. Employees must make reasonable efforts to schedule treatments to minimize disruption. 9. Employee Responsibilities · Provide timely notice and documentation. · Maintain communication with HR during leave. · Continue health insurance contributions. 10. City Responsibilities · Provide eligibility notice within 5 business days . · Maintain confidentiality of medical information. · Ensure non-retaliation and compliance with FMLA. 11. Enforcement Violations of FMLA rights may result in disciplinary action and legal remedies. Employees may file complaints with HR or the U.S. Department of Labor.

Text.Content:
No, FMLA cannot be used to care for a pet undergoing surgery. FMLA leave is only available for specific qualifying reasons, such as:

- Birth and care of a newborn child.
- Placement of a child for adoption or foster care.
- Care for a spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition.
- The employee’s own serious health condition preventing job performance.
- Qualifying exigency related to a family member’s military service.
- Military Caregiver Leave to care for a covered service member with a serious injury or illness [1].

CitationSources.Name: FMLA Policy.docx
CitationSources.Text: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) Policy 1. Purpose This policy ensures compliance with the Federal Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) and provides eligible employees of the City of Evergreen with job-protected leave for qualifying family and medical reasons. It also outlines employee rights, responsibilities, and procedures for requesting leave. 2. Eligibility Employees are eligible if they: · Have worked for the City for 12 months (not necessarily consecutive). · Have completed 1,250 hours of service in the preceding 12 months. · Work at a location where the City employs 50 or more employees within 75 miles . 3. Qualifying Reasons for Leave Eligible employees may take up to 12 weeks of unpaid leave in a 12-month period for: · Birth and care of a newborn child. · Placement of a child for adoption or foster care. · Care for a spouse, child, or parent with a serious health condition. · Employee’s own serious health condition preventing job performance. · Qualifying exigency related to a family member’s military service. Military Caregiver Leave: Up to 26 weeks to care for a covered service member with a serious injury or illness. 4. Definitions · Spouse: Includes common-law and same-sex marriages. · Child: Biological, adopted, foster, stepchild, legal ward, or a child for whom the employee stands in loco parentis. · Serious Health Condition: Illness or injury requiring inpatient care or continuing treatment by a health care provider. 5. Notice Requirements Employees must: · Provide 30 days’ notice for foreseeable leave. · Notify HR as soon as practicable for emergencies. · Submit required documentation (medical certification, adoption papers, etc.). 6. Benefits During Leave · Health insurance continues under the same terms. · Employees may use accrued paid leave concurrently with FMLA. · Job restoration to the same or equivalent position upon return. 7. Request Process 1. Complete the FMLA Request Form (see Appendix A). 2. Submit supporting documentation within 15 days . 3. HR will confirm eligibility and respond within 5 business days . 8. Intermittent or Reduced Schedule Leave Permitted when medically necessary. Employees must make reasonable efforts to schedule treatments to minimize disruption. 9. Employee Responsibilities · Provide timely notice and documentation. · Maintain communication with HR during leave. · Continue health insurance contributions. 10. City Responsibilities · Provide eligibility notice within 5 business days . · Maintain confidentiality of medical information. · Ensure non-retaliation and compliance with FMLA. 11. Enforcement Violations of FMLA rights may result in disciplinary action and legal remedies. Employees may file complaints with HR or the U.S. Department of Labor.

Text.Content:
**Definition of Child under FMLA**

A **child** under the Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA) includes:
- Biological child
- Adopted child
- Foster child
- Stepchild
- Legal ward
- A child for whom the employee stands in loco parentis (in the place of a parent) [1].
